subject: The truth of the matter

Opponents of voting rights are betting we'll ignore certain states| | | Fighting in every state that's trying to suppress voters doesn't mean we're going to ignore the big purple states that get all the attention, Team. But **the truth of the matter is that the time and resources spent to protect voting rights are not evenly split across states** , harming some of our fellow citizens because they live in one state instead of another. **[It's on us to fix that and ensure we're fighting for your ability to cast a ballot everywhere. | | Team -- Opponents of voting rights have made a strong bet. They've doubled down on it the last few years, too. Their bet was that we'd only care about certain states. That as long as we were focused on the swing states like Arizona, Georgia, Pennsylvania, and others, they would be free to suppress votes in states like Utah, South Dakota, Mississippi, or Kentucky. And you know what? **That bet has paid off for them.** When Wisconsin tried to ban drop boxes, there was an immediate reaction and lawsuits. When South Dakota did the same? _Crickets_. When Texas started messing with changes to voter ID requirements, people howled. When Utah functionally suppressed the youth vote with new ID requirements? _Got away with it with no consequences_. **They've created a feedback loop:** States with restrictive voting laws receive little attention because they aren't electorally competitive. But part of the reason they aren’t competitive is because _their state laws prevent large segments of the population from voting_. **[Team, we cannot afford to skip any state when it comes to protecting our fundamental right to vote. Step up and give $25 or whatever you can today and help us make every voice heard in our elections>>](https://click.everyaction.com/k/109227999/550413870/1723810624?amtOpts=&recurringAmtOpts=&am=&recurringAm=&is_optimized_ask=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5MjE1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImE2ZThkODg1LWM1NDQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=LccZ8Uq1G9ZvLuulIW7lDQaM_lQVGKCjDxgYDHdJcjA=&emci=0650c948-9744-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=a6e8d885-c544-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=33603084)**

| | We're not going to lie -- **when we've highlighted things in these things in the past** , like voter ID in Utah or court decisions in Kansas or the most suppressive state-level voting law in Indiana, we haven't gotten as much of a response as we did from swing states. **The danger in ignoring the voting rights of citizens in more than half the country is that it creates a two-tiered democracy**. Voters in the "swing states" benefit from attention and legal actions, while voters everywhere else are left to navigate increasingly onerous and burdensome requirements without meaningful support or scrutiny. We have to fight everywhere. **Donald Trump is actively dismantling democratic institutions and undermining the rule of law**. Only through free and fair elections can we hope to restore our republic. But that requires free and fair elections in every state, not just in a strategic few. **[Fuel our efforts to fight for voting rights everywhere with $25 or more and make every vote count>>](https://click.everyaction.com/k/109228006/550413877/1723810624?amtOpts=&recurringAmtOpts=&am=&recurringAm=&is_optimized_ask=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5MjE1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImE2ZThkODg1LWM1NDQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=LccZ8Uq1G9ZvLuulIW7lDQaM_lQVGKCjDxgYDHdJcjA=&emci=0650c948-9744-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=a6e8d885-c544-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=33603084)**

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.

Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. [**Your support is crucial to our efforts. clean_body: Opponents of voting rights are betting we'll ignore certain states| | | Fighting in every state that's trying to suppress voters doesn't mean we're going to ignore the big purple states that get all the attention, Team. But the truth of the matter is that the time and resources spent to protect voting rights are not evenly split across states , harming some of our fellow citizens because they live in one state instead of another. | | | Team -- Opponents of voting rights have made a strong bet. They've doubled down on it the last few years, too. Their bet was that we'd only care about certain states. That as long as we were focused on the swing states like Arizona, Georgia, Pennsylvania, and others, they would be free to suppress votes in states like Utah, South Dakota, Mississippi, or Kentucky. And you know what? That bet has paid off for them. When Wisconsin tried to ban drop boxes, there was an immediate reaction and lawsuits. When South Dakota did the same? Crickets. When Texas started messing with changes to voter ID requirements, people howled. When Utah functionally suppressed the youth vote with new ID requirements? Got away with it with no consequences. They've created a feedback loop: States with restrictive voting laws receive little attention because they aren't electorally competitive. But part of the reason they aren’t competitive is because their state laws prevent large segments of the population from voting. If you've saved your payment information with FastAction, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | | We're not going to lie -- when we've highlighted things in these things in the past , like voter ID in Utah or court decisions in Kansas or the most suppressive state-level voting law in Indiana, we haven't gotten as much of a response as we did from swing states. The danger in ignoring the voting rights of citizens in more than half the country is that it creates a two-tiered democracy. Voters in the "swing states" benefit from attention and legal actions, while voters everywhere else are left to navigate increasingly onerous and burdensome requirements without meaningful support or scrutiny. We have to fight everywhere. Donald Trump is actively dismantling democratic institutions and undermining the rule of law. Only through free and fair elections can we hope to restore our republic. But that requires free and fair elections in every state, not just in a strategic few. | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8) **[BREAKING FROM _AXIOS_ : “Trump admin rolls back LGBTQ+ gains during Pride Month”](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61911?t=3&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8)** **OFFICIAL FIGHT FOR REFORM POLLING ** [**Does your city have a Pride Parade?**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61911?t=4&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8) [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61911?t=5&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8) | | | [YES ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61911?t=6&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8)

| [NO ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61911?t=7&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8)

---
*We are conducting this poll to see which cities are continuing to have Pride Parades despite Trump’s continued attacks on the LGBTQIA+ community. Your participation is critical. [Please respond before 11:59 PM 06/08/25.](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61911?t=9&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8)
Look– Pride Parades have been an essential part of the LGBTQIA+ Movement and provide a sense of community and support nationwide.

But TRAGICALLY, Donald Trump and his cronies are attacking the LGBTQIA+ community. They are trying to roll back discrimination protections, ban gay marriage, and MORE.

It is MORE important than EVER that cities across the United States stand up for their LGBTQIA+ communities.

**That’s why we need Good Democrats like you to fill out[our survey](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61911?t=10&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8) so we know for sure...**
**[Does your city have a Pride Parade?](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61911?t=11&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8)**
| | [YES ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61911?t=12&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8)
---
| [NO ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61911?t=13&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8)
---
[UNSURE ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61911?t=14&akid=23123%2E4449941%2Epc6LH8)
---
Thanks,

Fight For Reform

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 6/8/2025
body:
From LA riots to migrant crime—why Colorado should be paying attention
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**June 8, 2025** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST** **By Heidi Ganahl**
| | Dear Readers,
What’s happening in California right now should set off alarm bells here in Colorado.
This week, [riots broke out in Los Angeles](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) after ICE arrested multiple violent migrants with long criminal records—some charged with sex crimes against children and known gang affiliations. The chaos turned deadly serious: attacks on officers, federal buildings torched, and a total breakdown of law and order.
President Trump responded by [federalizing 2,000 National Guard troops](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)—and warned that Marines could be next if the violence continues. Governor Newsom called the move “deranged.” But the reality is, the breakdown in public safety isn’t a political stunt—it’s a national crisis.
So why does this matter to Colorado?
Because we’ve seen the same warning signs here. From Boulder to Denver, Colorado cities have embraced sanctuary policies that tie the hands of law enforcement and prioritize ideology over safety. We’ve covered stories of parolees reoffending, of migrants released back onto our streets, and of a legislature more interested in “rights for all” than rule of law. At what point do we ask: who is protecting the law-abiding citizen?
This isn’t fearmongering—it’s a reality check. When law and order collapse in one state, it sets a dangerous precedent for all. California is just a few steps ahead in the progressive playbook that’s already creeping into Colorado.
As federal officials revealed this week, some of the very migrants being protected by sanctuary policies were never screened properly to begin with. That’s not compassion. That’s negligence.
At Rocky Mountain Voice, we’ll keep asking the tough questions. Who’s benefitting from the chaos? Who’s paying the price? And most importantly—how do we push back before Colorado finds itself in the same spiral?
We can’t afford to be passive. As citizens, we must be alert, informed, and ready to act when our state veers off course.
Stay sharp. Stay bold. And stay in the fight.
In liberty and truth,
Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[Ganahl: Congress must back DOGE to cut waste and energize GOP voters for 2026](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Under President Donald Trump, the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE)—led by Elon Musk—is lighting a fire under Republican voters ready to rip out Washington’s waste at the roots.

**[O’Donnell: One in 20 workers is a state employee—who’s footing the bill?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Communism, socialism, Marxism, Maoism, post-Mao Chinese-ism, and fascism may wear different uniforms, but they all march to the same beat—state control. One-party rule, diminished freedoms, political prosecutions, judicial overreach, hostility to markets, and the slow suffocation of private enterprise under the weight of public bureaucracy.

**[Kalam: Colorado’s Woke Zealotry Ushers Islamist Terror into Boulder](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Just over a week ago, I warned that the shooting of two young Israeli embassy employees in Washington, D.C., by a far-left zealot chanting “Free Palestine” was not an isolated act but a harbinger.

|
---
**STATE**
**[Sixth wolf death of 2025 confirmed—CPW says ‘wolf population will continue to grow’](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[DENVER7](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Another gray wolf that was brought to Colorado as part of the state’s reintroduction program has died, Colorado and federal officials said on Monday afternoon.

**[10 most dangerous states to live in revealed: Colorado comes in at #2](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[U.S. NEWS & WORLD REPORT](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Both violent crime and property crime declined in the U.S. in 2023, according to estimates from the FBI, with the rate of murder and nonnegligent manslaughter dropping by about 12% and the burglary rate down by roughly 8% year over year. The rate of vehicle theft, meanwhile, rose by 12%.

**[New rules take effect July 1 for Colorado gun owners seeking concealed carry permits](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[FOX 31 NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) A law changing the requirements for concealed firearm carry permits will go into effect on July 1, and gun owners should be aware of what is changing.

_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[Massive 19-state operation shatters Chinese sex-trafficking networks](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE BLAZE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Chinese organized crime is fueling a $5 billion-per-year sex-trafficking empire in the United States, operating numerous illicit massage parlors where 75,000 victims are enslaved and traumatized.

**[DOJ: Anti-Israel protester on Columbia University campus had direct Hamas connection](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[NEW YORK POST](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) A “Jew-hater” who protested against Israel on Columbia University’s campus and contemplated setting a student on fire allegedly had a direct link to Hamas’ deadly al-Qassam Brigades militant group, The Post can reveal.

**[Two investigations signal Education Department’s pivot from Pride Month to Title IX enforcement](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[DAILY WIRE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) President Donald Trump’s Education Department has declared June “Title IX Month” in recognition of the 53rd anniversary of the historic legislation’s passage, The Daily Wire has learned.

**[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[“Write your own rules?” Douglas County voters to decide if it’s time for Home Rule](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) On June 24, Douglas County voters will weigh in on whether to create a Home Rule Charter Commission – and decide who should serve on it. The commission would be made up of 21 members, including six from each commissioner district and three at-large.

**[“Not one mention of sexual abuse”: Parents slam Jeffco union as Title IX storm builds](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Jeffco Public Schools is at the center of a storm as teachers and parents clash with Superintendent Tracy Dorland over leadership, transparency, and the district’s handling of cultural and safety concerns.

**[Fremont rancher’s cattle remain quarantined after 15 die mysteriously—county and state investigating cause](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[KOAA](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) A rancher in Fremont County continues to seek answers as to why 15 cows died in one day. News5 first reported the incident on March 21. Since then, the rancher, Kerri Higgs, has been scrambling to learn the cause of the deaths from several tests.
**_[READ MORE LOCAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_**
|
---
**COMMENTARY**
**[Montrose Commissioner Pond: Why I’m Drawing the Line and Standing Against Federal Land Grabs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _The views expressed here are my own and do not represent an official action or position of the Montrose Board of County Commissioners. I was appointed to represent the people of District 3 in Montrose County, and I took an oath to uphold the Constitution. That includes defending our land, our rights, and our way of life here in Western Colorado._ __
**[Garbo: Boulder wasn’t a clash, it was terrorism](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _What happened in Boulder was an act of terrorism, plain and simple. The alleged actions of Mohamed Sabry Soliman, targeting Jews in what authorities have rightly identified as an ideologically motivated, antisemitic attack, must be condemned in the strongest possible terms._
__
**[Devotional: The breastplate of righteousness isn’t a costume—it’s combat gear](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Reflecting on the lives of remarkable figures—Abraham Lincoln, Nelson Mandela, Mother Teresa, Billy Graham, etc.—we discover a golden thread that weaves through each of their stories: character._
__
**[READ MORE COMMENTARY](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming a RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [tori@rockymountainvoice.com)](mailto:tori@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\)) or send us a message on social media to chat!
|
---
**☸️ DID YOU KNOW?** The **world’s oldest wooden wheel** is over 5,000 years old. Discovered near Slovenia’s capital in 2002, this ancient wheel—now displayed in a local museum—dates back over 5,000 years, making it the oldest known of its kind.
|

The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!

|

subject: Think about this, Friend:

#
Friend, **Only 2% of Congress comes from a working-class background.** Think about that: while folks in the Central Valley stretch every dollar, and make tough choices to get by, the people making our laws are mostly millionaires who’ve never had to struggle. **That’s why this campaign matters.** I didn’t grow up with privilege or connections – I grew up watching my parents fight to keep the lights on and food in the fridge. I helped out however I could, learned the value of a dollar early, and understood struggle before I understood politics. I know what it’s like to do everything right – to work hard, play by the rules, and still face roadblocks at every turn. That experience shaped who I am, and it’s exactly what’s missing in Congress right now. This campaign is about flipping the script – and flipping the seat. Together, we can shatter that 2% ceiling – and show what working-class power really looks like. clean_body: We just launched our 2025 Voter Outreach Fund. | | = Big news, friend: We’ve launched our 2025 Voter Outreach Fund: = = | | = In a race as competitive as ours, we have to connect with as many folks as possible early on. We have to act now to shore up enough support to send Marlene to Congress. The bottom line is this: chipping in now helps us later. = = Marlene is ready to be a true voice for hardworking Arizona families, but we’re relying on your support to help get her elected. = = Thank you for acting now, — Team Marlene | To learn more about Marlene, visit: We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to PAID FOR BY MARLENE FOR CONGRESS Marlene for Congress 7904 E. body:
Has it really come to this, Friend?

Our entire 2025 Voter Verification Polling Team has tried reaching YOU.

Senator Ted Cruz has reached out.

Kellyanne Conway has reached out.

Chairman Tim Scott even tried to contact you.

_**[But now, with our verification deadline nearly here, we're PLEADING with you!](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/GEg0iOWsS5KBi8u9XqIIHA~~/AAVLBhA~/bAKCMJNcpzi3sin2FEkxrYPf27L_tWHQ7IFlrfhABrKq0yzCplRz-owUht77YSWCRgdl1CotkMe7iOrQ8Wlnnn2OM6-KA-tMtY_t-s9_2YwUFCw9UjNA-eGOoDCSY1WqRPmMzHVANRLlUFJWdPD2txeonGHTKFXdiZcbcG46XxGEN-H8Nn3-6QxOoKTAB3oCGBXSt_qGpfKZUG6ibMGQhNWc7TJNmT4hwmjvc2N21usGy8tFaKlOOecMVlnC680pnB4M5Kn7KvkQKDjIzwHu0jT9tiaGj_N8Y0AJSVLTJkHUSRHwljDHVzwYmj7JCPX6IHO4h5tMKLX22c-X6n8CpTC_q0RoZuojP_sIRPXiRzCV8kAWpzNPM0JoadqTQe4mbLcp1ExFNebR1qW_UrTuZw~~)**_

Here's something I'm proud of:_I'm living our values as I represent our district._
I’m focused on helping our region recover from wildfire damage, pushing back against the Trump administration’s devastating cuts to public services, and ensuring our residents get the support they’ve earned – in their retirement, their healthcare, and more.
**I hope these efforts make it clear that nothing is more important to me than the people I serve.**
Of course, that means a lot less time for fundraising than most of my peers. Truthfully, I wouldn’t have it any other way; but it makes moments like this, as we near our major FEC deadline, that much more critical.
Your early support will help keep a progressive, community-focused leader in Washington to fight for you, and give us the resources to help like-minded allies in tough races down the stretch.
That’s why I’m asking you:
[**With our FEC fundraising deadline coming up, will you help ensure we have the power to flip the House blue and continue to achieve big things in Washington – for Glendale, and for everyone who calls CA-30 home? name: Elizabeth Nader
subject: TOMORROW is Election Day
body:
Peter, I know you may be tired of me saying this, but it is WAY too important not to remind you one more time... TOMORROW is Primary Election Day in the Garden State.
| | [ ]()
---
Peter, I know you may be tired of me saying this, but it is WAY too important not to remind you one more time... **TOMORROW** is Primary Election Day in the Garden State. If you vote, you’ll have a say in deciding which candidates end up on our November ballot – and which ones are sent home. If you don’t vote, the elite establishment gets to make that choice. Do we want to leave it up to them? So do whatever you need to do to get to your polling place tomorrow and cast your vote. Stop to vote on your way to work, or on your lunch break, or after picking your kids up from school.
---
**🕙 Election Day voting locations are open from 6 AM to 8 PM.** **Find your Election Day polling place[here](https://xtz223.keap-link014.com/v2/click/58b4ce11d903d7104bbdc4057c565426/eJyNkEGLwjAQhf_LnGtjW12hNxGRUvWwrGcJ7aDRmIRkbHWl_93prnhS8Prmm_fmzQ0IjTRU1JDDhX7TNIMIPFbKKTQ0s4Zk9TdNx6MkSyLQyhwX3p4d5LeXy0-glyfjSRoBXR0y8vM9nZXFerFdFuuSSSc9Z3zk85Ulw6fPfDUtltB1b43xpGjesHeAnPwZ-0a14la08Zr5PZELuRBt28bmEO9sIwJJQoGaGWVNEI0lHDir-YrdQNtK9nIc9nTSHCCdQ1M_vlPi9T-muwNKR28t) 👉**
---
[ **Show Me Election Day Polling Locations** ](https://xtz223.keap-link014.com/v2/click/71f06182841a88b60e5677e7f8ef9cb9/eJyNkEFvwjAMhf-Lz6VZWxhSbwghVJVxQNt5ilqrBEISJaYdoP73uYA4gcT1-fN7fr4AoZGGihpy-KNzmmYQgcdKOYWG5taQrK7TdDJOsiQCrcx-6e3RQX55uvwABnk6mY4joJNDRr43s3lZrJe_q2JdMumk54y3fD6z5OPhs_iaFSvo-5fGeFC0aNk7QE7-iEOjWnEr-vGa-S2RC7kQXdfFZhc3thWBJKFAzYyyJojWEo6c1XxFM9K2koMchy0dNAdI59DU9--UeLrF9P9MHW8v)
---
And make sure to stay in line. It’s up to us, friends. Let’s save New Jersey together. Forward this email to 5 friends, grab your phone, ID, and keys, and let’s go vote out some lemons! For New Jersey, Elizabeth Nader
---
| [ ]()
---
President
Common Sense Club
---
|
---
Hello, and thank you for taking the time to read this email. This message is part of an ongoing conversation between us, and I want to take a moment to ensure you have full transparency about why you received this message, how your information is handled, and what to expect from future emails. Why You’re Receiving This Email You’re receiving this email because at some point, you opted in to receive updates, news, or information on a specific topic we’ve previously discussed or shared. Whether it was through a subscription, a form submission, or another form of communication, your information was shared willingly, and we respect your decision to connect with us. If you’re wondering about the nature of our correspondence, rest assured that we aim to keep all emails relevant, timely, and free from unnecessary clutter. This includes respecting your inbox and refraining from sending irrelevant messages. Your Privacy is Important We value your trust and take your privacy very seriously. The information you provide is securely stored and never shared, sold, or used outside the purpose for which you provided it. If you ever want to review how we handle your data, feel free to contact us directly. Transparency is our priority, and we’re happy to address any questions you might have. You are always in control of the information you share. If you feel that any part of your subscription or interaction needs clarification, let us know. We’re here to provide accurate answers and ensure your satisfaction. How to Unsubscribe or Manage Your Preferences We understand that everyone’s inbox is different. If you ever find our emails no longer relevant, there’s no hard feelings. You can easily manage your email preferences or unsubscribe using the link provided below. By clicking the unsubscribe link, you’ll be taken to a page where you can either adjust your communication preferences (such as receiving fewer emails or only on specific topics) or completely remove yourself from our list. The process is straightforward, and any changes will take effect promptly. We don’t use tricks or gimmicks to keep you subscribed. Our priority is to ensure that our emails add value to your day, and if they don’t, we respect your decision to part ways. Accessibility and Communication If you have any trouble accessing the unsubscribe page, managing preferences, or understanding why you’re receiving this email, you can reach out directly to our support team at info@commonsenseclub.org. We aim to provide a response within a reasonable time frame and address your concerns effectively. Our communication is designed to be as inclusive as possible, but we know there’s always room for improvement. If you have any feedback on how we can make our emails more accessible or relevant, please don’t hesitate to share. A Commitment to Non-Intrusive Emails We aim to create a non-intrusive communication experience. This means we won’t overwhelm your inbox with excessive messages, and we work hard to ensure our content remains clear and concise. The purpose of this email is to stay connected with you and provide updates or information that we believe is meaningful. If we ever fail to meet these standards, we encourage you to let us know. Feedback, whether positive or constructive, is always appreciated. Your thoughts help us understand how we can do better and improve our communication approach. While we cannot guarantee every suggestion will be implemented, we will take the time to carefully review and consider your input. Contact Information If you’d like to get in touch with us outside of managing your preferences, here’s how you can reach us: Email: info@commonsenseclub.org
Phone: (866) 916-3221 (please note this is a text-only line)
Mailing Address:
Common Sense Club
165 Route 35
Red Bank, New Jersey, 07701 We strive to ensure all communication channels are open and readily available to you. Whether it’s a question, comment, or concern, our team is ready to assist. Legal Information We comply with all applicable laws and regulations regarding email communication. This includes adhering to anti-spam laws and maintaining the highest standards for consent-based communication. Your trust matters, and we work diligently to ensure every email you receive meets these requirements. If you need further clarification on our compliance policies or legal obligations, please feel free to ask. Transparency and accountability are central to our communication strategy, and we’re happy to provide additional details if needed. A Final Note Emails are one of the many ways we stay connected, but we understand they aren’t perfect for everyone. If there’s another way you’d prefer to communicate or stay updated, let us know. Whether it’s through social media, a direct call, or another channel, we’re open to finding the most effective way to share information with you. We want to emphasize that our goal is never to disrupt or clutter your inbox. Every email sent is intended to provide value, and we genuinely appreciate your time and attention. Thank you for allowing us to stay in touch with you. This footer was designed to ensure transparency, provide essential information, and give you full control over your communication preferences. We hope it meets your expectations, but if there’s anything you’d like to see improved, we’re always here to listen.
---
[Unsubscribe](https://xtz223.infusionsoft.com/app/optOut/0/4fd6eb57577aacb0/28021000/4c71ecd09360b419) Common Sense Club 165 Route 35 Red Bank, New Jersey 07701 United States
---

name: Wiley Nickel
subject: It’s early
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wiley Nickel for North Carolina**
body:
| Hi Peter, I know it's early, but I wanted to take a moment to share some thoughts as we start our day. As I pause to reflect on the state of our nation, North Carolina, and the race ahead, I'm reminded of the strength and resilience of the people who call this great state home. Each day presents its challenges, but it's the tenacity and determination of North Carolinians that fuels my commitment to serving and fighting for positive change. In many ways, we’re standing at a crossroads, grappling with issues that impact our daily lives and the very fabric of our democracy. From threats to voting rights to assaults on health care access, these challenges demand action. North Carolina has felt the weight of these struggles, with politicians like Thom Tillis standing by as our rights are eroded and crucial needs are sidelined in favor of partisan agendas. Tillis has repeatedly chosen to align himself with extremist views and the interests of billionaires, rather than standing up for the everyday citizens of North Carolina. _It's a pattern we cannot afford to let continue, and I’m determined to fight against it with everything I've got._ The truth is, we have an opportunity — a responsibility — to flip the Senate and ensure that it works for the people, not for the entrenched special interests. This race is about more than just a political seat, it's about restoring the values of democracy and fairness. It's about championing those who have been ignored, punished, and sidelined for far too long. Peter, as we move forward together, let's remember the power of collective action and the importance of standing up for what is right. North Carolina deserves leaders who will fight for them, who will listen, and who will never back down from challenging the status quo. We all do. Thank you for being part of this journey. Our path ahead is clear, and together, we can create the change we need. As our day unfolds, remember the progress we've made together. Let's continue this important work. I hope you have a great day! Wiley | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109229941/550453769/474294398?refcode=AU_GWN_CL_GEN_NOA_AC_20250609_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTk2ZTc4MS0xODQ1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QGcemwsb4PHhy1Rf7HOE4Of2r2bmsAwjMinNJU8Tn1Y=)

| | | | | Peter, Donald Trump is taking aim at NPR by signing an executive order that would immediately defund our beloved programming! NPR has brought free and impartial news, educational programming, and entertainment to millions of homes across the country. But Trump, Musk, and his MAGA cronies are hellbent on ELIMINATING programing like NPR because it dares to hold them accountable to the public!

We can't just stand idly by as the GOP takes aim at free and independent press. But we need to check in with 5,000 Top Democrats with a live poll: Do you support NPR?

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T242bc842-c650-4a69-8cff-9b0a69334972/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Mini Solar Generator
subject: Never Fear Blackouts Again: Discover the 8-Pound Solar Generator that Defies Blackouts
body:
For the First Time Ever you can get this breakthrough NEW solar device...
| | | [](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21564/6374453/85721f7316ccaa107cf8ddbb8dd2a093578fa9c3/afad0e47995a1798fbd322440a3f5935cc403213)
---
| | |
---
_June 9th, 2025_
---
| | |
---
| | _**The Future of Solar Energy Is Here**_ **Breakthrough 8-Pound Solar Generator Is The Ultimate Protection Against Dangerous Blackouts And Toxic Fumes**
---
[](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21564/6374453/85721f7316ccaa107cf8ddbb8dd2a093578fa9c3/afad0e47995a1798fbd322440a3f5935cc403213)
---
A dangerous storm strikes...
And in a flash...
Supplies vanish in your city.
Grocery stores... EMPTY.
Water... SOLD OUT.
Batteries... SOLD OUT.
Gas stations... SOLD OUT.
And without gas, that means no gas generators to keep your lights on, and no way to protect yourself and your family during a dangerous black out.
Entire neighborhoods in darkness...
**But picture this...**
Instead of panicking and fumbling around in the dark, fearful and without supplies like everyone else...
Imagine you and your family sitting back, relaxing and enjoying your evening...
Riding out the storm and the power outage in total safety and with total peace of mind.
---
[](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21564/6374453/85721f7316ccaa107cf8ddbb8dd2a093578fa9c3/afad0e47995a1798fbd322440a3f5935cc403213)
---
All because you have the [**Patriot Power Sidekick**](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21564/6374453/85721f7316ccaa107cf8ddbb8dd2a093578fa9c3/afad0e47995a1798fbd322440a3f5935cc403213), a revolutionary, mini solar generator that powers all your important devices, keeps lights on, **never needs gas, and costs less than an iPhone!** Well you can! For the **FIRST TIME EVER** you can get this breakthrough NEW solar device... That harnesses the limitless and FREE energy of the sun, and that's **safe to use inside** because it's fume-free, silent and never needs gasoline to run. But if you want one, you better hurry... Because customers have been practically begging for this product to hit the market. And now that these next batches of units are ready to ship from Utah, USA... these mini solar generators won't be around for long. You're among the first to know about... **The NEW Patriot Power Sidekick
Finally... a Mini Solar Generator
that Never Needs Gas and that
Costs Less than an iPhone!**
---
[](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21564/6374453/85721f7316ccaa107cf8ddbb8dd2a093578fa9c3/afad0e47995a1798fbd322440a3f5935cc403213)
---
[**Order Today and Get $243 of FREE Gifts!**](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21564/6374453/85721f7316ccaa107cf8ddbb8dd2a093578fa9c3/afad0e47995a1798fbd322440a3f5935cc403213) Small and mighty, the Patriot Power Sidekick is your personal "portable power plant" that can power your phones, laptops, medical devices, radios and more! You'll be able to run a toaster oven, a CPAP machine through the night, your TV, radios, GPS devices, lamps, small personal space heaters, fans, a mini fridge, electric blankets... **And you can use it right inside your home, because it's fume-free and SAFE.** You can't say that about a typical generator. Not by a long shot. So you can have the ultimate peace of mind that you and your family will stay connected when disaster strikes.
* Silent, Fume-FREE & Safe
* Fast Recharge
* Long-Lasting Battery
* Recharge 2,000 Times!
* Easy to Use
* Completely Portable & Easy to Use!
* Powers Your Favorite Devices
* FREE Portable 40-Watt Solar Panel Included

**and more!** That's why it's our privilege to introduce to you:

**[The Patriot Power SidekickIs An Essential Part ofYour Preparedness Plan!](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21564/6374453/85721f7316ccaa107cf8ddbb8dd2a093578fa9c3/afad0e47995a1798fbd322440a3f5935cc403213)**
---
[](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21564/6374453/85721f7316ccaa107cf8ddbb8dd2a093578fa9c3/afad0e47995a1798fbd322440a3f5935cc403213)
---
_"I really like how many things the Sidekick can run. I can charge my cell phone and iPad at the same time, run an electric razor, my radio, an electric blanket and so much more. It's so easy to take with you anywhere – very light!"_ _Craig W.
Police Officer_ [**➡ YES! I Want to Protect My Family Today With Life-Saving Solar Power!**](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21564/6374453/85721f7316ccaa107cf8ddbb8dd2a093578fa9c3/afad0e47995a1798fbd322440a3f5935cc403213) **More Raving Reviews From Customers** **Rita L.** This product is so versatile, and at only 8 lbs, my grandchildren can get it. We had many trees down from a storm. The guys kept having to refill their gas powered chainsaws. My husband came out with this "little" box and his electric chainsaw. They laughed and said a long electric cord would be too dangerous. My husband calmly hooked up his Sidekick and made short work at lopping off the branches and then walked through the path he made. It lasted longer than the gas 'polluting' machines. Very grateful you have made this available to buy at an affordable price. **Steve G.** Peace of mind for years. I purchased this product to provide power to run a box fan in the summer and lights in the winter. I can use this product every day for over four years as it can be recharged up to 2,000 times. It won't make noise that draws attention and can create comfort in a difficult situation. Best of all, it's portable. **Thomas B.** Saved my CPAP machine during the winter storms. I just survived the ice storm, the freeze and the power outage in Texas using my 4Patriots products. I was most happy about my Patriot Power Sidekick. It's quiet. It's clean. I was able to deploy the solar panels on the dashboard of my van, it charged up. And then that night I plugged in my CPAP machine and it ran it all night long. With enough to charge my phone in the morning. **Jana H.** Portable power was a blessing when the grid failed us. I didn't think I'd need this but I needed it shortly after receiving it. This past February was the worst freeze that we, in Texas, have seen in a long time. Our power grid failed big time. We were without power for a couple of days. This product was a blessing. We were able to have a light and a small portable TV. I highly recommend this product and the company that sells it. **Allan D.** Patriot Power Sidekick to the rescue. We brought our new Patriot Power Sidekick with us on our last camping trip and it was a blessing. Our primary use was to power an air pump to easily fill up our air mattress for a comfortable night's sleep. One night we had a pinhole leak in the air mattress, so we just kept the Sidekick inside the tent hooked up to the air pump and ran it for a few minutes about every 3 hours to keep it fully inflated. It's quiet and powerful, and we will have it with us on every camping trip! **Chris S.** Power out, no problem. Recently had a power outage due to some crazy storms. I use my phone for everything. Having the sidekick charged up and charging my nearly dead phone meant I was able to have my alarm and get to work on time. Thank you for this compact, lightweight and fume free portable charging/light station available for when I needed it the most! **Richard B.** Well worth it! This is one of the items that made me a loyal customer. I never was a big fan on solar-powered anything. At the same time I've been in enough situations to know any type of a generator is necessary when you live in a more rural setting. Especially when gas prices go through the roof. I purchased the Sidekick and as soon as it arrived I unpacked it and plugged it into a lamp and left it plugged in all evening. I was impressed how little it took from the battery! After 3 hours the Sidekick was still at 98% fully charged. I'm looking forward to having two Sidekicks! **Frank S.** We have been looking for quite a while for something like this. The deal with the solar panels included was something not to pass up! We both have sleep apnea and use c/bipap machines for sleep. The power went out recently and the sidekick worked great. As a test we ran the Sidekick on the humidifier and it ran about 7 hours! It's a small humidifier. That's not bad for that type of application. Cannot wait till camping season comes along! We highly recommend this product. [**➡ YES! I Want to Protect My Family Today With Life-Saving Solar Power!**](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21564/6374453/85721f7316ccaa107cf8ddbb8dd2a093578fa9c3/afad0e47995a1798fbd322440a3f5935cc403213)

clean_body: For the First Time Ever you can get this breakthrough NEW solar device... | | | | | | June 9th, 2025 | | | | | The Future of Solar Energy Is Here Breakthrough 8-Pound Solar Generator Is The Ultimate Protection Against Dangerous Blackouts And Toxic Fumes A dangerous storm strikes... And in a flash... Supplies vanish in your city. Grocery stores... EMPTY. Water... SOLD OUT. Batteries... SOLD OUT. Gas stations... SOLD OUT. And without gas, that means no gas generators to keep your lights on, and no way to protect yourself and your family during a dangerous black out. Entire neighborhoods in darkness... But picture this... Instead of panicking and fumbling around in the dark, fearful and without supplies like everyone else... Imagine you and your family sitting back, relaxing and enjoying your evening... Riding out the storm and the power outage in total safety and with total peace of mind. All because you have the , a revolutionary, mini solar generator that powers all your important devices, keeps lights on, never needs gas, and costs less than an iPhone! Well you can! For the FIRST TIME EVER you can get this breakthrough NEW solar device... That harnesses the limitless and FREE energy of the sun, and that's safe to use inside because it's fume-free, silent and never needs gasoline to run. But if you want one, you better hurry... Because customers have been practically begging for this product to hit the market. And now that these next batches of units are ready to ship from Utah, USA... these mini solar generators won't be around for long. You're among the first to know about... The NEW Patriot Power Sidekick Finally... a Mini Solar Generator that Never Needs Gas and that Costs Less than an iPhone! Small and mighty, the Patriot Power Sidekick is your personal "portable power plant" that can power your phones, laptops, medical devices, radios and more! You'll be able to run a toaster oven, a CPAP machine through the night, your TV, radios, GPS devices, lamps, small personal space heaters, fans, a mini fridge, electric blankets... And you can use it right inside your home, because it's fume-free and SAFE. You can't say that about a typical generator. Not by a long shot. So you can have the ultimate peace of mind that you and your family will stay connected when disaster strikes. Silent, Fume-FREE & Safe Fast Recharge Long-Lasting Battery Recharge 2,000 Times! Easy to Use Completely Portable & Easy to Use! Powers Your Favorite Devices FREE Portable 40-Watt Solar Panel Included and more! That's why it's our privilege to introduce to you: "I really like how many things the Sidekick can run. I can charge my cell phone and iPad at the same time, run an electric razor, my radio, an electric blanket and so much more. It's so easy to take with you anywhere – very light!" Craig W. Police Officer More Raving Reviews From Customers Rita L. This product is so versatile, and at only 8 lbs, my grandchildren can get it. We had many trees down from a storm. The guys kept having to refill their gas powered chainsaws. My husband came out with this "little" box and his electric chainsaw. They laughed and said a long electric cord would be too dangerous. My husband calmly hooked up his Sidekick and made short work at lopping off the branches and then walked through the path he made. It lasted longer than the gas 'polluting' machines. Very grateful you have made this available to buy at an affordable price. Steve G. Peace of mind for years. I purchased this product to provide power to run a box fan in the summer and lights in the winter. I can use this product every day for over four years as it can be recharged up to 2,000 times. It won't make noise that draws attention and can create comfort in a difficult situation. Best of all, it's portable. Thomas B. Saved my CPAP machine during the winter storms. I just survived the ice storm, the freeze and the power outage in Texas using my 4Patriots products. I was most happy about my Patriot Power Sidekick. It's quiet. It's clean. I was able to deploy the solar panels on the dashboard of my van, it charged up. And then that night I plugged in my CPAP machine and it ran it all night long. With enough to charge my phone in the morning. Jana H. Portable power was a blessing when the grid failed us. I didn't think I'd need this but I needed it shortly after receiving it. This past February was the worst freeze that we, in Texas, have seen in a long time. Our power grid failed big time. We were without power for a couple of days. This product was a blessing. We were able to have a light and a small portable TV. I highly recommend this product and the company that sells it. Allan D. Patriot Power Sidekick to the rescue. We brought our new Patriot Power Sidekick with us on our last camping trip and it was a blessing. Our primary use was to power an air pump to easily fill up our air mattress for a comfortable night's sleep. One night we had a pinhole leak in the air mattress, so we just kept the Sidekick inside the tent hooked up to the air pump and ran it for a few minutes about every 3 hours to keep it fully inflated. It's quiet and powerful, and we will have it with us on every camping trip! Chris S. Power out, no problem. Recently had a power outage due to some crazy storms. I use my phone for everything. Having the sidekick charged up and charging my nearly dead phone meant I was able to have my alarm and get to work on time. Thank you for this compact, lightweight and fume free portable charging/light station available for when I needed it the most! Richard B. Well worth it! This is one of the items that made me a loyal customer. I never was a big fan on solar-powered anything. At the same time I've been in enough situations to know any type of a generator is necessary when you live in a more rural setting. Especially when gas prices go through the roof. I purchased the Sidekick and as soon as it arrived I unpacked it and plugged it into a lamp and left it plugged in all evening. I was impressed how little it took from the battery! After 3 hours the Sidekick was still at 98% fully charged. I'm looking forward to having two Sidekicks! Frank S. We have been looking for quite a while for something like this. The deal with the solar panels included was something not to pass up! We both have sleep apnea and use c/bipap machines for sleep. The power went out recently and the sidekick worked great. As a test we ran the Sidekick on the humidifier and it ran about 7 hours! It's a small humidifier. That's not bad for that type of application. Cannot wait till camping season comes along! We highly recommend this product. | | | MATERIAL CONNECTION DISCLOSURE: You should assume that the sender of this email has an affiliate relationship and/or another material connection to the person or businesses mentioned in this message and may receive commissions from purchases you make on subsequent web sites. ## Runoff Elections Decided
Runoff elections for several high-profile mayoral and city council races were held on Saturday. In San Antonio, former Under Secretary of the Air Force [Gina Ortiz Jones](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O3CAUhZ8Gd4zgGvNTUKRxt2lTWhguOyQYLGDXO28fzUjJC2x3pE86Ot_59ba9uZRTef_pDrTjK9at4NUzjoFtax-lxogZ_Ui19DO5Uv3dHecGDBbKJGVmCy7lxyamYHcWDJ_QciWMkFLrZbpb0LPgzmgfBDAR1RzmBXcNAQzuwegp2WcZk8xwzjjwm5beO-G52pXnzEgiGIY0aruNOw78cj3mR_3EdvP1mLK9j3F2Mv8gsBJYr-u6_cHSlyclsLo2ks9IYH2KEVjPmtNI_hn_mxFYuyvUlVFLqvRwj9roP0ob9o88Ov1dC3Z6uj3XTl9DCKygZhpE9OAlUGWkpsJEQ7Xfgcao1CK8YMuupmbDeaWcU5eKCPZ-uJRfBuN7v39a-BsAAP__L9aXCg) defeated former Texas Secretary of State Rolando Pablos 54% to 46% to become the city’s next mayor. The Democratic National Committee said that Jones overcame “massive amounts of right-wing dark money.” In McKinney, real estate agent and former city council member Bill Cox defeated pastor and former Republican State Rep. Scott Sanford 53% to 47% in the race for mayor. Cox is a proponent of [expanding McKinney National Airport](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL3OozAURJ_GdBfZDhhcuEjDVtl2pW2QsS_BWv8g2wnw9qvkFb5upJFGc86fx_zQzrv4_K0DqnquaY54FI-1Yp7zK6Z1RY-muhTL7nRMZtNhnznlPVABVM5WO3_NXWPVQq1kDSo2dLITYhz7ZlO9Zb2WlvFVW2ms6eltsIwPo-gtlwIbpz5jVFDJGGWctaMwRneGDctgGJWCdBStqym3dcOKpy6rv9Ibc2tSaLzaat0Lud0JnwifjuNoQ4mfjvAJI7wK4dMHivDpmxfnPZh0gkdtXXxCTbCgSQEhmH8uRrygQMSzQtBXyoA6-wsylpevBcqWDsInneF-Z78cDX-brOx-OO9dEQPp6DNo57_n6s-UvhX_HwAA__8aeIzM), while Sanford wanted to reassess the project. In Frisco, Burt Thakur ousted incumbent Tammy Meinershagen 55% to 45%, becoming the [first person of Indian descent](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuwyAYhJ8Gb1hAbBwGhizulE5VO1p_8E9AxWABjpO3r5KqT9DxdNLpvu_rPJ3BBx-v77Cgrnebpoh7CVgr5ilvMVmLAU31KZbVQ0zGwbJOgomeMkmZmmbw4TF1zawvbFa8Qc2HTnVSHo9947QFPoAdsDsotCDFBSTyHtRgZa-UkY3XzzEmmeKcccHbozQGOsOHy2A4U5J0DGdfU26rw4p3KDY80g1za9LSBO1qXQs5nIgYiRj3fW-XEp8dESNGuhUixicUEWOqDjMRY3XwvWWKAWa6biFQcAgzRcjhQX2kNvtiEv3lp38C6Ct64yHQjGULtdDi0k7ECJmeTvzNfeaPJut53X0IvsiBdOy6gA-vq_V_gm9a_AQAAP__7gSTBw) to win a city council seat. Meinershagen’s campaign was plagued by a secret recording in which she asserted the local Indian population hadn’t sufficiently integrated into the community.
## Hazing, Assault Allegations at Catholic School
San Antonio attorney Jesse Guerra is [calling for a Texas Rangers investigation](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuhDAQRL_GdCDbGAOFizR0lzYl2jPrw9JiI7w57v4-IvmEdFOMRvPe122-QaSYHp-woeNXyHPCsxAy4zEf3ymHgISeY05lj5CyX2HbZy11V0tby3FeINJ7NtXi7nIZVYVO9WY01g5DV61OBYNhWELrle18UKNte_TSWD3e5aC6KrprTFo5KiWVVs1gvQfjVX_vvZKjFUbiEjkfDa_I-IIS6J2feDQ-bxW5lXkvov0QehJ6Os-z2d4FEiTOKearJPR0UQk9UfZAQk9wcPSEQk8eEx9AtQdeM0Vfx_TEwvEBF3MNRPgXS61lazvdyWZf9-pwy35GolhsL4x8bBDp9xD_T-PT6Z8AAAD___4xiPo) into allegations of bullying, hazing, and sexual assault at Central Catholic High School. Administrators [expelled two students](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDHPnDAMhn8NbDklKRdgYOjC9nXtiHyJc1g1CSK-g_v3VTp1_xbLei350fP-_lq-gJjS8xdsOMkV85LwLIwieCzHK-UYkdEL5VR2gpT9Ctu-WG3vSjulxyUA8Wfp2jA9dBhNi5Ppu7Fzbhju7ToNLgYb7g7AgR_jY4gBwmhdDzoa04eWpvpMOz0ao401t8F5D503_aP3Ro-u6TQGknzcZEXBC0rkT37jcfN5a3laRfbS_PjZ2Lmx83metz8FpB4bO1ebxs41UZTeWISeIFizim3srF0ddbOqyCtgkqLw2pEZg4pH3pTHJAew8iBrZvIqZuZ8UnqqgtcLWG1UfE7h5f-nUE6q-DVnVgU-FdkeU9hPYqbi-qbTzw2I_2nI98p_T_ZvAAAA__9OZZ0g) following the report of the allegations in April, and they are hiring an independent firm to investigate, as more alleged victims have come forward. Guerra claims he could compile up to 10 sworn affidavits from current and former students for a pending lawsuit regarding the [alleged actions](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0b2O3CAUxfGnwR0jwHyYwkUad5s2pYUv1wblDliGXe_k6aPJK6Q-0pF--v_6WD9CplyOn-GJc__e61rwboS947Ven6XuOxJCz7W0M4dSIYXnuSqhDBeWC7_GkOm16iHOm4heDjhLp722dprMkGbp7CYdYJRoYN_j7kdlcARnvPLG6CHP7zNhhZdSSCUfkwUIGqTbHEjhLdMCY-71evSEHb9D2-lVv_B6QH0ONKfez8bGH0wtTC33fT9-Y2nmvTK1hKtnIGRqecOYWqhCIKaWFs7IIWWKPJeIgAVeHLD0KxCH0FOlDDzlI_EGqVbiLRQeSq8lV57Cn1wOvn0SvXI5mFqUG_kkxqgnZ7lzFrnWQnIfjefbNI3CjN5I3IZrjuediXKzjmlxPEOmf5T-fwG-ZvU3AAD__2--mV0) of several soccer team members at the prestigious all-boys school. The school has asked parents and students to report incidents of bullying or hazing and has encouraged families to [contact local police](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL3OnDAQRZ8GOiPbYQ0ULtJst2lTosEMeCTjQfbsX54-2n2Fr73F1Tnn722-ASXK-x840Mtr4znjsyYUwTKXe-Ztw4RBiHM9CTKHCMc5W20vSjulp3kFSu-5b1e_6HUyLXoz9FPv3Dhe2ugDgIPN6GGxYxi1cbiGZYKLmZZlMNvYkv-caacnY7SxphtdCNAHMyxDMHpyTa9xJeHSSUTBF9QtvfmBpQt8tMlHkbM2v3439trYa4UMWTgTFzy5SMdl_8z4ukNSEf5R3hWkhDt8pRTlBwmqGspdKL8VbypglgJJBZDIiYKKtEdVQ2ROjb22xa_nk1Ki6oam1_sBlL4w8rOED2__BwAA___BYopf) about claims of assault.
## Asian Needle Ants Spreading
The invasive Asian needle ant, in the U.S. since the 1930s, is [spreading in Texas](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ8GOpDtLAYXLv4mXf72SuTY67DSYiPjC8nbn4LuDa77NCN9I83Xbb45YkqP_25FW18xzwmPnbFWLHP5TjlGZPSVcto3cin7xa3brIQaOqE7YebgiN8ztMHeRTCyRStHMKD1NA3tYu8wGLgEGOMYvFHgQMLkvdM6KsDJtGQ_MqGFkVJIJftJe-_Ay_E-eimMbkBgoJpLXxes-HJ75Hd-Yul9Xlu2S63b3lz-NeraqOtxHL1fSk6ftlFXpoifmDj8oiuVPJ-0k0tdQgyMnUu1O-2dEhcNw2D6bdnaYsN2EDPtemxAPFZHfA7Xv931tOonAAD__66zfss). Its arrival is raising concerns about its potential threat to native ecosystems, not to mention its painful sting. The dark brown to black ant is often mistaken for native ants, but can be identified by its slender waist and stinger. They nest in [moist, shaded areas](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOtCAUhZ9GOwzwM6gFxd_YzbZbmitc9CYIBhidefvNzD7C1iffyTnf932-AwWK6xfsaOrTpzniVQLWinnOj5i8x4C2UorlIIjJbrAfs-TyxrhmfJwdUHjNqnVm4W4ULRrRq1FpPQy3djNewOg19F6pHno7OIfWWy5A2UF5sbRk3mVc81EILqToBm0tKCv6pbeCj7pRHB3VlLu6YcUnFB9e6cTc2bS3wWy1HqX597-RUyOn67o6n57vF--8kdOGEOrWyIniCYVOZA7iijk9CvMpI62RUSxoK6tbRqgYKa7seCyBLPulGeyYyUL8EKWWNht3XBQCFd03iq87UPgMqn_TeBr5EwAA__85zYxf). Once established, they compete with and displace native ant species critical for seed dispersal. While not as aggressive as fire ants, their sting is quite painful and can cause allergic reactions. "_Imagine somebody inserting a needle directly into your flesh_ ," [said Benoit Guénard](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGy4yAMhk-DOzKC2GAXFNu4e9tu6VFAxMwS8AB5jm-_w17hNVIjfdL3__navjDEkJ6_8UWmfXzeEp01UmtUtvJO2XuKZFvIqR4BU7Y7vo5Ngpw4KA7L5jDEaxsHZx7gFjGQEXpcRqXmeRp2Q6MX3uKEWis7Pe5OeJjEIhzctdcPOwTTYaBgEQKEFLdZWYujFfqhrYBFsRHIhZbLre3U6IPVxyt_U7nZ_Bqi2Vs7Krv_YnJlcj3P8_au2LLDqw8wudaWy8Xk2s16w27D5NrvMrmC6mXuu8H-JcexBkw8EblIHFOrvB6F0IX05GhLrpW3nfi7w-YR5CLlDKCZXIdi3HGGGENVmo3wfGGI_99sPwv328h_AQAA__8iJYzN), a professor of ecology and entomology at the University of Hong Kong. Researchers at Rice University are tracking the spread of Asian needle ants throughout the state. “ _We’re just starting to understand how it fits into our local ecosystems_ ,” [said associate professor of biosciences Scott Egan](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykks1uozAQx5_GuTkyhphw4JBt4jZbpVWzq35dkLEH4sXYBJuP9OlXVNon2MscfhrNb0bzfzsVJ6GNtvWTaCEPc-UKC5M3EAL0RT9YV1VgQAbtrO-0sE5eRNsVlNANJgyTrFBCm1uRrFReEpVFK8ijNMkSxrbbzeqSx4xWZBPFLFFExSyBUiTlVookEiotU7LS-TKMMJJFEYlotN4yKUUio7RMZUQyhhICSgfXr8MFAszCV-bmRujX0rUrk19C6DyKd4hyRPk0TWtptGzoxQ0-OLt0IcqXsxDlxklhEOWLE1FO2FISRLm2o_B6BCy8FhZbAGUACxuw73oQStsaa4u__RjmDvrg8SR6i12FO6FtNRjsg7a1xyCdv_kALe61bxYtivkQ2sK7oZeA4n0lJJTONYiyhbeg9NCieO-d1Mt-31SKthO6tgu36h90NoANKN43XS8poqwqpdEKxfvj9EHnP2J6HG_XnwYezsOOclO-HOfTyw8tW9McfvNQGnN3fjXz8xv_fD7wCe6j0-6cBvf4cJedX8by-P6wL_FVnQ6_aOPlNat8jZNbf-u7a62_trvtE1fWmeHj_kPY3bUuBLTFhjaXT_UeR_w0vn6x491hZvtp1eeqm7Qx2rMUJaRuhTbffwv_l7Yxp38DAAD___Im714).

In San Antonio, former Under Secretary of the Air Force defeated former Texas Secretary of State Rolando Pablos 54% to 46% to become the city’s next mayor. The Democratic National Committee said that Jones overcame “massive amounts of right-wing dark money.” In McKinney, real estate agent and former city council member Bill Cox defeated pastor and former Republican State Rep. Scott Sanford 53% to 47% in the race for mayor. Cox is a proponent of , while Sanford wanted to reassess the project. In Frisco, Burt Thakur ousted incumbent Tammy Meinershagen 55% to 45%, becoming the to win a city council seat. Meinershagen’s campaign was plagued by a secret recording in which she asserted the local Indian population hadn’t sufficiently integrated into the community. Hazing, Assault Allegations at Catholic School San Antonio attorney Jesse Guerra is into allegations of bullying, hazing, and sexual assault at Central Catholic High School. Administrators following the report of the allegations in April, and they are hiring an independent firm to investigate, as more alleged victims have come forward. Guerra claims he could compile up to 10 sworn affidavits from current and former students for a pending lawsuit regarding the of several soccer team members at the prestigious all-boys school. The school has asked parents and students to report incidents of bullying or hazing and has encouraged families to about claims of assault. Asian Needle Ants Spreading The invasive Asian needle ant, in the U.S. since the 1930s, is . Its arrival is raising concerns about its potential threat to native ecosystems, not to mention its painful sting. The dark brown to black ant is often mistaken for native ants, but can be identified by its slender waist and stinger. They nest in . Once established, they compete with and displace native ant species critical for seed dispersal. While not as aggressive as fire ants, their sting is quite painful and can cause allergic reactions. "Imagine somebody inserting a needle directly into your flesh ," , a professor of ecology and entomology at the University of Hong Kong. We are closing the gap to not only defend but EXPAND President Trump’s majority – and the Left is terrified. Fellow patriot, we’ve crunched the numbers and with our razor-thin majority now within the margin of error, YOUR voter profile is the difference maker. THAT’S WHY I’M REACHING OUT TODAY TO SIMPLY ASK THAT YOU VERIFY YOUR GOP VOTER PROFILE TO GIVE ME AND PRESIDENT TRUMP THE MOST ACCURATE POLLING POSSIBLE AS WE CLOSE THE GAP ON THE DEMOCRATS. PLEASE COMPLETE THE QUESTIONNAIRE BEFORE THE DEADLINE TONIGHT AT 11:59 P.M. TO VERIFY YOUR VOTER PROFILE. >> [] [] VERIFY NOW [] Thank you, [] Jim Jordan VERIFY NOW [] Paid for by Team Jordan a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed between Jim Jordan for Congress, Buckeye Liberty Fund, and American Liberty Foundation PAC. # [The Future Economy](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=165482670&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1NDgyNjcwLCJpYXQiOjE3NDk0NzA3ODAsImV4cCI6MTc1MjA2Mjc4MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.u79AeaKvCUyNrTxSfljwQW991HmMoNcFzX_bC_Otpy4)

---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=165482670&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1NDgyNjcwLCJpYXQiOjE3NDk0NzA3ODAsImV4cCI6MTc1MjA2Mjc4MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.u79AeaKvCUyNrTxSfljwQW991HmMoNcFzX_bC_Otpy4)

---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/07d4747d-19cb-4f3d-93fb-f6df9b9b6fdd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
What will the economy of the future look like?
I get asked this a lot, and the truth is, for most people . . . not great.
Dario Amodei, the CEO of Anthropic, recently came out and [said](https://substack.com/redirect/03973dbd-dbfe-402a-864c-90003bf1d472?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that entry-level white-collar work - the work formerly done by new college graduates - will be wiped out and that unemployment could surge to 20% in just a few years. Dario is proposing an AI tax, so he's not trumpeting a case that helps him. About 44% of American jobs are categorized as either "Routine Manual" or "Routine Cognitive" by the Federal Reserve and could thus be theoretically automated away.
Over 2 million young people graduated from college last week. I'm not sure what today's 22-year olds are going to do in five years, and college is somehow twice as expensive as when I graduated. I think to myself all of the time, "I'm glad I was born when I was." I got to come of age before social media arrived and started depressing teenagers. And I had the chance to gain professional experience back when you could just show up and demonstrate your ability.
Could you just show up today? A lot of routine cognitive jobs are middle-class jobs that will disappear. Economists call this "job polarization" which is when you have jobs at the bottom and the top and not as many in-between. Some jobs are going to be inconvenient or not worth it to automate - like cleaning a hotel room - and are going to be with us a long time. But those aren't going to be jobs that people are going to be pumped to do.
If you've been following me for a while, you know I've been convinced this would happen since 2018 when I wrote _[The War on Normal People](https://substack.com/redirect/6cba6af0-3f04-4bc7-a6a1-f0a862a4acfe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_. I was in Iowa talking about self-driving trucks back then. Now, the first robot trucks are hitting the highways. It's been a busy 7 years.
I'm totally confident this is unfolding because I talk to friends who tell me what they're working on. One founder I know told me he's looking at replacing 2,000 customer service workers with AI. This isn't some jerk, he's a good guy and you've heard of his company. An entrepreneur said that he'd replaced his entire design department with AI. Having staff used to be a sign of corporate health and growth. Today it increasingly means that you're not doing it right.
Coding used to be the most secure career path; now AI is writing code faster than any human could. One experienced coder with AI is now able to replace entire teams. I know of several people who were laid off from their tech companies recently. There are even stories of former coders becoming delivery drivers.
A [study](https://substack.com/redirect/92933ccf-1af0-4387-bffc-412c3a8b8691?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) came out recently saying that 60% of Americans now can't afford a minimal quality of life. AI is going to supercharge inequality and gut middle-tier jobs, making a bad problem even worse.
This isn't to say that there won't be areas of positive impact. There will be massive advances in biotech, energy, quantum computing and more. Diseases will be cured and drugs will be discovered. But the average American is unlikely to feel those benefits in their own lives; they're going to wonder where their next rent check is coming from and what their kids are going to do.
If you accept that humans are going to lose to technology across the labor market, then you start thinking, "What are people going to do when they wake up in the morning?" Jobs and job-like arrangements are positive for people in terms of community, structure, purpose, fulfillment and development. The goal should be to build and retain as many arrangements like that as possible. The absence of that is already having negative effects across the country; think men staying at home without much to do.
The vision I've been driving, which I wrote about in _[The War on Normal People](https://substack.com/redirect/6cba6af0-3f04-4bc7-a6a1-f0a862a4acfe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ is a human-centered economy where we value people's time and ability across a whole range of activities. One stepping stone in that direction is a system called Timebanking, which enables people to barter services based on what they can do. For example, if I tutor your child I could get a Time Dollar, that I then give to someone else to give me a ride. That person could then give it to someone else to help cook a meal. And on and on. Everyone has something they can offer. This was proposed in Edgar Cahn's book, _[No More Throw Away People](https://substack.com/redirect/29995a37-8303-493f-adbb-b8d5e992fd27?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ , and has been adopted by dozens of communities.
Last year I met with Stephen Dubner of Freakonomics who had recently read Cahn's book. He and I decided to collaborate to try to catalyze a timebanking implementation. We identified an initial Executive Director, Jenny Kassan, who was already working to help entrepreneurs in Baltimore. I [interview](https://substack.com/redirect/07d4747d-19cb-4f3d-93fb-f6df9b9b6fdd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Jenny this week to talk about her work.
The economy is a man-made creation based on various rules and incentives. If we follow the current rules, millions of people will quickly become irrelevant as AI doubles in capacity every seven months. Despair will follow. Can we build an economy of the future that we are actually excited about for most people? The fate of Western civilization hinges on our ability to answer that question.
For my interview of Jenny Kassan, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/07d4747d-19cb-4f3d-93fb-f6df9b9b6fdd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). For the Freakonomics episode on Timebanking, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/70614f8d-7986-474d-8591-8f5a6e9abaff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). To give to the work in Baltimore, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/dd6acf2f-5624-4e92-ad2f-f897bf465839?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). To see what Forward is doing to advance our politics to start addressing these issues, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/c19c2d5c-8f11-4f88-a21a-950149ab661e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Forward got a lot of new members last week due to some prominent people talking about the need for a new party.

clean_body: What will the economy of the future look like? | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more | | ---|---|--- * | | | Jun 9 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- What will the economy of the future look like? I get asked this a lot, and the truth is, for most people . . . not great. Dario Amodei, the CEO of Anthropic, recently came out and that entry-level white-collar work - the work formerly done by new college graduates - will be wiped out and that unemployment could surge to 20% in just a few years. Dario is proposing an AI tax, so he's not trumpeting a case that helps him. About 44% of American jobs are categorized as either "Routine Manual" or "Routine Cognitive" by the Federal Reserve and could thus be theoretically automated away. Over 2 million young people graduated from college last week. I'm not sure what today's 22-year olds are going to do in five years, and college is somehow twice as expensive as when I graduated. I think to myself all of the time, "I'm glad I was born when I was." I got to come of age before social media arrived and started depressing teenagers. And I had the chance to gain professional experience back when you could just show up and demonstrate your ability. Could you just show up today? A lot of routine cognitive jobs are middle-class jobs that will disappear. Economists call this "job polarization" which is when you have jobs at the bottom and the top and not as many in-between. Some jobs are going to be inconvenient or not worth it to automate - like cleaning a hotel room - and are going to be with us a long time. But those aren't going to be jobs that people are going to be pumped to do. If you've been following me for a while, you know I've been convinced this would happen since 2018 when I wrote . I was in Iowa talking about self-driving trucks back then. Now, the first robot trucks are hitting the highways. It's been a busy 7 years. I'm totally confident this is unfolding because I talk to friends who tell me what they're working on. One founder I know told me he's looking at replacing 2,000 customer service workers with AI. This isn't some jerk, he's a good guy and you've heard of his company. An entrepreneur said that he'd replaced his entire design department with AI. Having staff used to be a sign of corporate health and growth. Today it increasingly means that you're not doing it right. Coding used to be the most secure career path; now AI is writing code faster than any human could. One experienced coder with AI is now able to replace entire teams. I know of several people who were laid off from their tech companies recently. There are even stories of former coders becoming delivery drivers. A came out recently saying that 60% of Americans now can't afford a minimal quality of life. AI is going to supercharge inequality and gut middle-tier jobs, making a bad problem even worse. This isn't to say that there won't be areas of positive impact. There will be massive advances in biotech, energy, quantum computing and more. Diseases will be cured and drugs will be discovered. But the average American is unlikely to feel those benefits in their own lives; they're going to wonder where their next rent check is coming from and what their kids are going to do. If you accept that humans are going to lose to technology across the labor market, then you start thinking, "What are people going to do when they wake up in the morning?" Jobs and job-like arrangements are positive for people in terms of community, structure, purpose, fulfillment and development. The goal should be to build and retain as many arrangements like that as possible. The absence of that is already having negative effects across the country; think men staying at home without much to do. The vision I've been driving, which I wrote about in is a human-centered economy where we value people's time and ability across a whole range of activities. One stepping stone in that direction is a system called Timebanking, which enables people to barter services based on what they can do. For example, if I tutor your child I could get a Time Dollar, that I then give to someone else to give me a ride. That person could then give it to someone else to help cook a meal. And on and on. Everyone has something they can offer. This was proposed in Edgar Cahn's book, , and has been adopted by dozens of communities. Last year I met with Stephen Dubner of Freakonomics who had recently read Cahn's book. He and I decided to collaborate to try to catalyze a timebanking implementation. We identified an initial Executive Director, Jenny Kassan, who was already working to help entrepreneurs in Baltimore. I Jenny this week to talk about her work. The economy is a man-made creation based on various rules and incentives. If we follow the current rules, millions of people will quickly become irrelevant as AI doubles in capacity every seven months. Despair will follow. Can we build an economy of the future that we are actually excited about for most people? The fate of Western civilization hinges on our ability to answer that question. | Who Are We Endorsing for Lieutenant Governor?
---
| Friends-
There is just a little over a week until Virginia's primary election, and with federal job cuts, tariffs, and rising unemployment rates, it's more important than ever to vote for an affordable Virginia. That's why we're proud to endorse Senator Aaron Rouse for Virginia's Lieutenant Governor. Senator Rouse is fighting to make our tax code fairer and make medicine more affordable by taking on big drug companies and Pharmacy Benefit Managers.
---

name: James O'Keefe
subject: Meet the Man the Elites Fear
body:
I’ve seen what they’re capable of. That FBI raid? They treated me like a terrorist, all to protect their own. The lawsuits? A flood meant to drown me. But I’m still standing because this matters—because you deserve to know how they’re gaming you.

| [****](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zohhs8op416adco7ni9bac4hiia09op44fvjg098_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s5geapr6c5h2bs)
[****](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z6m5631qicp9hl6a1cl9udnim6qjaj34s35kkik0_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s5geapr6c5h2bs) Dear Patriot,

I'm James O'Keefe, and I founded OMG (O'Keefe Media Group) to do what the mainstream won't: expose the powerful with raw, unfiltered truth. We caught IRS officials on hidden cameras, bragging about their AI overreach--how they're building a surveillance net to track your every dollar. _The Post Millennial_ called it "chilling," and X users said it's "the kind of journalism we've been starving for." That's OMG: no fluff, just facts, straight from the source.

But this work comes at a cost. I've been jailed on trumped-up charges, had my home raided by the FBI over Ashley Biden's diary, and faced endless lawsuits from the left. Why?

Because I won't stop. I don't have a personal life--just a relentless drive to uncover corruption. I've built OMG from the ground up to fight for people like you, [**and now we need**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z43u02vscgcag6ra08s10r3dra2jqtno3ltiebpo_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s5geapr6c5h2bs)**[**$64,600 by June**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zefs7jod19blcmmoum19i8ac7v253rb2p1ociceg_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s5geapr6c5h2bs)**[**to keep going. Will you join us?**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zvjmptucki0ogst4bnfbdmg8l926d2591p7bq7ug_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s5geapr6c5h2bs)

**People call OMG a "wrecking ball" against the establishment. **

When we dropped the IRS tapes, it wasn't just a story--it was a warning shot.

Supporters say we're "rewriting the rules of journalism," while critics whine we're "too aggressive." Good. **The truth isn 't polite**.

But fighting the system takes resources--cameras, lawyers, guts--and we're running low. [**Your support keeps us in the game, digging into the shadows the elites want hidden.**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zklgq14giiholgmmh846r0hak9c5afhlqspemfc8_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s5geapr6c5h2bs)

I've seen what they're capable of. That FBI raid? They treated me like a terrorist, all to protect their own. The lawsuits? A flood meant to drown me. But I'm still standing because this matters--because you deserve to know how they're gaming you.

OMG's got more exposes ready to drop, [**but**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2znkabe57iktne2lihl7pi8rd6o8h25i1gmuvjp18_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s5geapr6c5h2bs)**[**$64,600 by June**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z5vha5q9afs9mha6dm0o6eeu8di7gk671tqc3k08_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s5geapr6c5h2bs)**[**is our lifeline. Without it, we fade, and they win.**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z7iv7nj0islr6u78acgi0fpgv9mthad1cr9c8ob8_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s5geapr6c5h2bs)

If you're sick of the lies, the cover-ups, the smug insiders laughing at us, then this is your fight too. [**Your donation today --$20, $100, whatever you can--fuels work the mainstream's too scared to touch**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zk018frbloga0ltfsrk0j3u8pm7hg14rgihlo7bg_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s5geapr6c5h2bs).

I'm not here to play nice; I'm here to win. Thank you for considering this. **With you, we 're unstoppable.**

In Truth,
James O’Keefe
Founder and CEO
O'Keefe Media Group
